title: CIP Header Schema
description: JSON Schema for validating YAML frontmatter headers in Cardano Improvement Proposals (CIPs). Most field-level value rules are enforced by the validation script with friendly messages; the schema enforces structural/type constraints.

CIP.description: The CIP number (positive integer, without leading 0), or a placeholder before being assigned: a single quoted "?", 'unassigned', 'pending', or 'TBD'. Value-level checks (positive, no leading zero) are enforced by the script.

Title.description: A succinct and descriptive title. Length cap enforced by the script.

Category.description: One of the editorially accepted categories covering one area of the ecosystem

Status.description: Status of the CIP: Proposed, Active, or Inactive with a required parenthetical reason. Format enforced by the script.

Authors.description: A list of authors' real names and email addresses (e.g. John Doe <john.doe@email.domain>). Per-entry format enforced by the script.

Implementors.description: A list of implementors committed to delivering an implementation, formatted as 'Name <email-or-URI>' per entry (same shape as Authors); the bracketed contact may be an email address or a project URI. 'N/A' when not applicable; [] when there is no implementor yet. Per-entry format enforced by the script.

Discussions.description: A list of links where major technical discussions regarding this CIP happened. Must include a link to the pull request that created the CIP. Each entry must have a label followed by a colon and URL. Can be a string 'Label: URL' or a dictionary {'Label': 'URL'}.

Solution To.description: A list of CPSs that this CIP addresses, if any. Omit the field when not applicable. Each entry is 'CPS-NNNN[?] [| optional title]: URL' (string) or {'CPS-NNNN[?] [| optional title]': URL} (dict). Bare 'CPS-NNNN' requires a tree/blob URL to that CPS folder; 'CPS-NNNN?' requires a pull-request URL. Per-entry semantics enforced by the script.

Created.description: Date created on, in ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) format. Format enforced by the script.

License.description: Abbreviation of an approved license. Allowed value enforced by the script (with a did-you-mean hint on near-misses).
